title: Consistency in verbs in podman help command
body: Most of verbs used to describe different podman commands is written in 1st person, like "Attach", "Run". But for some commands it is written in 3rd person, like "Displays", "Umounts". This request changes those to 1st person verbs for consistency.

comments.body:
@bodunov Thanks for the PR.  You need to sign your PR.

git commit -a --amend -s
git push --force

Should fix.

We don't accept any patches that modify the vendored content.  IE anything under vendor directory.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *bodunov*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Can one of the admins verify this patch?

- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`

bot, add author to whitelist

> git commit -a --amend -s

> Should fix.
> 
> We don't accept any patches that modify the vendored content. IE anything under vendor directory.

Thanks. Done.

Regarding vendor stuff.. Curious how come one command "help" ended up in vendor directory and not in main code.

@bodunov Remove the vendor patch altogether, only have the first patch. You would need to convince urfave to accept your change and then once they merged, you would need to update vendor.conf and pull in the new version.

If we want to fix this by default, without vendoring, I think we need to implement the help options and ship our own within our code.

> @bodunov Remove the vendor patch altogether, only have the first patch. You would need to convince urfave to accept your change and then once they merged, you would need to update vendor.conf and pull in the new version.
> 
> If we want to fix this by default, without vendoring, I think we need to implement the help options and ship our own within our code.

vendor.conf has the following comment:

"# TODO: urfave/cli is not active anymore. We should transition to another library."

so, not sure if it is worth to continue using and updating it... Perhaps own "help" option within podman code makes more sense.

LGTM, thanks for the patch @bodunov 
FYI urfave, we're looking into moving to Cobra instead, so I'd not work to hard in the urfave code at the moment.

Yes remove the second patch and we can merge, and then fix the other issue when we move to cobra.

Sorry for dummy questions.. I guess you meant "two PR lines", not "to". But when I do rebase it shows no lines. Just like this:

noop
